nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; inhabits holes and crevices in rocky and coral reef substrates

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding mainly on crabs and fish in the wild. In captivity tends to accept fresh or frozen meaty foods such as silversides, shrimp, and squid; live food may be used to entice newly acquired specimens.

summary: A temperate moray eel from New Zealand and southern Australia, reaching around 90 cm. Generally considered suitable for intermediate-level marine keepers who can provide cool, well-oxygenated water and substantial rockwork.
careNotes:
The Green Eel (Gymnothorax prasinus) is a temperate species native to New Zealand, the Kermadec Islands, and southern Australia — cooler waters than most saltwater aquarium fish prefer. Maintaining appropriate conditions typically requires a chiller, with a target range of roughly 16–24°C. The species tends to spend daylight hours sheltering inside holes and crevices and is most active at night, so feeding in the evening is generally advised.

Like most moray eels, the Green Eel tends to be an accomplished escape artist, and a tightly sealed, weighted lid is strongly recommended. It tends to be aggressive toward smaller tankmates and small invertebrates, and is generally considered unsafe with crustaceans — crabs and shrimp form a natural part of its diet in the wild. Large, robust fish such as sizeable angelfish and tangs are the most practical companions.

Note: the Green Eel is sometimes sold as 'Yellow Moray' and should not be confused with the much larger Green Moray (Gymnothorax funebris), a Western Atlantic species that can exceed 2.4 m and requires a vastly larger system. Copper-based medications should be avoided, as moray eels lack true scales and tend to be sensitive to copper treatments.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Green Eel listed at $198.99; care level Moderate, Reef Compatible With Caution. The /products/green-eel detail URL returned data for Gymnothorax funebris (the large Green Moray), not G. prasinus — a possible catalog error. Product listing attributes (care level, reef compatibility) were used; sizing and range data deferred to FishBase.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Primary data source for confirmed scientific identification, max size (91.5 cm TL), native range (New Zealand, Kermadec Islands, southern Australia), temperature range (15.7–23.6°C mean 18°C), depth range (0–40 m), diet (mainly crabs and fish), habitat (reef-associated, nocturnal, stays in crevices), IUCN Least Concern. Species ID 10146. Also cross-checked G. funebris entry (250 cm, 23–28°C, W. Atlantic) confirming no data overlap with G. prasinus. BlueZoo Aquatics SSL certificate expired.
